Opposing militarism is the common will of the international community. 

Japan claims to be a pacifist and defense-oriented country, but its action says otherwise—rocketing military spending, export of lethal weapons, growing offensive capability, revision of its pacifist Constitution, and call for Japan to be a war-capable nation. The list goes on.

Japan has dropped the “pacifist country” mask. The international community must stay on high alert and never allow Japanese militarism to return and again menace the world.

Deep condolences over the passing of Mr. Yohei Kono, a dear old friend of the Chinese people known for his dedication to China-Japan friendship and commitment to historical justice. Our thoughts go out to his family.

In 1993, Mr. Kono, as Japan’s Chief Cabinet Secretary, officially acknowledged Japan’s responsibility over “comfort women” and expressed remorse and apology in the famous Kono Statement. Regarded as a flagbearer in upholding Japan’s pacifist Constitution, he once said, “Article 9 of the Japanese Constitution, which clearly stipulates that Japan renounces war and does not maintain war potential, represents both our determination, our resolve, and our ideal.”

On his deathbed, he still had China-Japan ties at heart and said in tears that he wished to make one last visit to China. Unfortunately, due to his worsening condition, Mr. Kono’s wish was not fulfilled.

We will always remember Mr. Kono, whose words are particularly relevant today: “History should not be forgotten, the conviction should be carried forward, and the commitments made should always be honored.”
items.summary:
